title: Papilionanthe subulata
intro: Papilionanthe subulata, also known as the golden orchid, is a species of orchid native to tropical Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Papilionanthe subulata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional medicines and supplements in various parts of Asia.
taxonomy: Papilionanthe subulata belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This species is a type of orchid and is part of the genus Papilionanthe. Papilionanthe subulata is native to Southeast Asia and is commonly found in tropical regions. It is a terrestrial orchid with unique characteristics, including its subulate leaves. The classification of Papilionanthe subulata is further supported by its membership in the tribe Vandeae within the subfamily Epidendroideae.
